business_description.nature_of_business: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy number WC GLFS 2025 8820 referenced on application; effective 06/01/2025

positives: 38 years in business with 25 years under current management
GPS tracking / ELD devices installed in all vehicles
MVR checks performed on all drivers at least annually
CDL licenses verified for all drivers at time of hire
Drivers in compliance with FMCSA Hours-of-Service regulations
Valid DOT number and MC authority maintained
concerns: No written contracts with all subcontractors
DOT violations or out-of-service orders occurred in the past 3 years
Most recent CSA BASIC score in Crash Indicator is above intervention threshold (currently being addressed)
Loss frequency: 10-13 claims annually over the past three policy years
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Prior 3 years of insurance: 2024 Hanover Insurance Group (10 claims), 2023 Hartford Financial Services (13 claims), 2022 Auto-Owners Insurance (12 claims). No cancellations reported. Prior premiums approximately $2.0M–$2.1M annually. Note: prior insurance section marked 'Need docs' by reviewer.

fleet_summary.radius_of_operation: Interstate (FMCSA) — Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors; sleeper-cab tractors used for long-haul operations

extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors. Primary commodities hauled: Dry van — general freight, packaged goods; Flatbed — steel, lumber, machinery. Hours of operation: Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 4:30 PM. 9 premises totaling approximately 730,000 sq ft across Ohio (Columbus, Independence, Toledo) and Pennsylvania (Canonsburg, Philadelphia, Harrisburg), including warehouses, a service/repair facility, and corporate office.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: Formal safety program in place. GPS tracking/ELD devices installed in all vehicles. MVR checks performed on all drivers at least annually. CDL licenses verified for all drivers at time of hire. Drivers in compliance with FMCSA Hours-of-Service (HOS) regulations. Valid DOT number and MC authority maintained (Interstate FMCSA). All premises sprinklered.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: DOT violations or out-of-service orders reported in the past 3 years. Most recent CSA BASIC score in Crash Indicator is above intervention threshold (currently being addressed). Prior insurance history shows 10-13 claims per year across last three carriers (Hanover 2024, Hartford 2023, Auto-Owners 2022) with no cancellations.
